You do not have what it takes tO remain in the sibko [Wash out of training) Your training otficer - takes keen interest" in your development [Wash out of training or take all of the following: EDG -1 Introvert; Addiction (2) ad Pain Resistance] You excel in the sims_ but the real thing secretly scares the hell out of you (+2 Skill Points t0 ay three Piloting ad/or Gunnery Skills, Combat Paralysis] Burned seriously in training accident [Unattractive_ Quirk / Fear of Fire Pain Resistance] You have rival [Enemy] incessant and brutal training turns you into ruth- less combat machine (CHA Introvert; +2 t0 any four of your Phenotype Field Skills] Your skills come t0 the attention of the leader of your Bloodname House (Contact (31. [Commission Rank 31, Good Reputation (2). Vehicle (6). Custom Vehicle (21] (Choose one event Or roll twice and apply both results reroll you get 10 and 11, as these events are mutually exclusive] STAGE 3: HIGHER EDUCATION In early adulthood; many people continue their education before entering the professiona worla _ Higher education can take many torms ranging from traditional college to military training This stage of the Life Path is optional; characters can proceed directly to Stage 4: Reai Life , depending on the play- er $ preference and the character'5 Next Path prospects Time: Unlike paths in the previous stages Higher Education paths take variable amounts of time, depending on the complexity of the courses or training; The number of years each path takes is listed under its Time heading: Repeating Paths: Characters can take some paths in this stage several times. Each repetition has different effects _ depending on the particular path. Characters cannot spend unlimited amounts of time in the Higher Education Stage , however. After completing Path this stage  character must proceed to the next stage (Real Life) and complete at least one patn there before return- ing to take another Higher Education path. This limit repre- sents tne tact that some affiliations courses Of study are expensive and the character'$ money will simply run out_ Even with state-funded schooling, the state will expect the character t0 enter (he workforce after an appropriate time_ in order to repay the state investment: Some Higher Education paths include subpaths which are considered part of the main path: For example_ Vehicle Technician Training is subpath of Technical College and is
available to characters who took the Civilian Tech Field Subpaths listed as part of this pass " can be taken immedi- ately following the preceding path or subpath, without going through Real Life. Alternatively; character can move on t0 Real Life and return to the subpath later: In either case character must meet the prerequisites t0 take the subpath Prerequisites for choosing certain occupational fields are itali- cized in parentheses next to the field. Continuatlon: Characters can continue in this stage as long as the Repeating Paths rules allow: After that, the character may proceed t0 Stage 4: Real Life (p. 471. If you choose t0 end the Life Path here and play your character as young adult; proceed to Assigning Attributes Traits and Skills (p. 54). Characters can also return t0 this stage after following one more Real Lite paths_ subject to any listed Next Path restrictions_ Clan Characters: Civilian characters ad failed warriors proceed to Stage for final training in their caste. Scientist caste characters follow the University Path; technicians the Technical College Path_ and merchants and laborers the Trade School Path (merchants must take the Merchant Field; labor: ers cannot):
MILITARY ACADEMY Cannot have Combat Paralysis Or ay of the following traits above the lowest possible Ievel: Disabled, Lost Limb Poor Hearing Poor Vision The finest warriors in the Inner Sphere graduate from the many military academies operated by the Successor States. Some have unique curricula and sO the following represents an average academy or participation in mercenary apprenticeship: Tnis path does not necessarily represent officer training_ but rather higher grade of training than that available through standard enlistment: Academy training differs from basic military enlistment mainly in granting access to addition- al fields of study. including MechWarrior and aerospace fighter pilot training After completing military training character must com: plete at least one Tour of Duty in the Real Life Stage.
